subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with labelled sections and a recognisable IntroductionâMethodâAnalysisâConclusion sequence
Concise and descriptive title and logical use of tables/figures to support the argument
weaknesses: Lengthy background and repeated explanations create verbosity and interrupt conciseness
Occasional abrupt transitions, pagination errors and uneven figure placement disrupt the flow
Inconsistent reference formatting and incomplete citation details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is organised with a clear structure and logical progression (stronger than basic), but unnecessary repetition, verbosity and some formatting errors prevent seamless flow. These qualities align most closely with band 2 for Presentation.

strengths: Use of LaTeXâtypeset equations enhances readability
Effective inclusion of multiple representations (scatter plots, box plots, regression line) to illustrate findings
weaknesses: Inconsistent mathematical notation (misâtyped subscripts, formula typos) and occasional informal language
Lowâresolution images and missing units/tick marks in tables and plots impair clarity
Figure and table numbering inconsistencies disrupt the readerâs navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs some appropriate mathematical language and a variety of representations, but inconsistent notation, clarity issues and informal phrasing limit communication to band 2.

strengths: Clear personal interest in Formula 1 drives the choice of topic
Evidence of independent thinking in manual calculations and model validation on new season data
weaknesses: Creativity and initiative are limited by reliance on standard statistical procedures
Personal approach could be deepened by proposing further novel extensions or alternative methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the studentâs genuine interest and individual dataset choice show some engagement, the exploration lacks the creativity or sustained initiative required for higher bands, placing it at band 1.

strengths: Reflects on the meaning of high correlation values and notes regulation changes affecting the model
Connects some findings back to the central research question
weaknesses: Critical analysis remains basic, with minimal discussion of causation, confounding factors or model limitations
Reflection does not explore statistical assumptions (e.g., heteroscedasticity, sampleâsize effects) or deeper critique
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides basic reflection and some link to understanding, but the discussion lacks depth and critical rigour, consistent with band 1.

strengths: Relevant courseâlevel mathematics (mean, standard deviation, Pearsonâs r, linear regression) used appropriately
Calculations are mostly correct with a solid conceptual grasp of statistical measures
Demonstrates awareness of census data collection by using all available teams and drivers
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic/typographical errors in slope and intercept calculations
Lacks transparent summation steps for variance and correlation computations
No quantitative error metrics (e.g., residuals, mean absolute error) included to assess model accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration applies appropriate, mostly correct mathematics at course level, demonstrating good understanding in parts. The presence of minor errors and missing detail keeps this at band 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing an individual data set, computing statistics manually and validating the model on 2020 data reveal significant independent thinking beyond a template exercise.

The student demonstrates good conceptual understanding of statistical measures, correlation and regression, yet deeper discussion of limitations or alternative models is missing, placing the work in âgood understanding in partsâ rather than âthroughoutâ.
Calculations are mostly correct; minor slips (e.g., slope formula mis-typed, rounding inconsistencies) are present but do not substantially impair results, matching âmostly correctâ.

Most material is relevant to the stated aim, but lengthy background on how races work and repeated explanations of points distribution create noticeable verbosity.  Hence the piece is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than fully concise.

Standard statistical symbols (Î¼, Ï, r) are used and formulae are typeset, yet there are slips such as writing $m = r\frac{\sigma_y}{\sigma_y}$ and inconsistent subscripts.  Language occasionally drifts into informal phrasing.  This indicates âsome appropriateâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ use.
Explanations of calculations are mostly comprehensible, but minor notation errors and low-quality image exports interrupt consistency.  Thus clarity is âmostlyâ but not fully consistent.
All mathematics (mean, SD, Pearsonâs r, linear regression) is at AI course level and directly relevant to the question, but it is not used with the sophistication expected for bands 4â6.

The student employs two scatter plots, four box plots, one regression line and supporting tables.  These cover multiple representation types and generally aid understanding, even if axes labelling and resolution could be better, matching the descriptor for âeffective useâ.
The student reflects on what large r-values imply, discusses outliers and considers regulation changes, indicating meaningful if not exhaustive reflection.

While the 2020-season test adds a modest original twist, the mathematical techniques remain standard and there is little evidence of novel methodology; creativity is therefore limited rather than âsignificantâ.

The investigation follows a recognisable IntroductionâMethodâAnalysisâConclusion sequence and arguments progress logically from data collection to statistical testing and model validation.  Although transitions are occasionally abrupt, ideas are seldom disjointed and the reader can follow the line of thought without major effort â qualities consistent with clear logical development rather than only âsomeâ coherence.

Critical discussion is present but basic: the exploration notes that salary correlation is weaker and that budgets will be capped, yet it stops short of deeper statistical critique (e.g., addressing heteroscedasticity or sample size).  This fits âbasic analysis with limited critiqueâ.
Reflections tie findings back to the central question and the student shows how the regression model can estimate future points, demonstrating a clear if not âstrongâ connection to understanding.

A contents page, labelled sections, numbered tables/figures and an appendix give the work a clear structure; introduction, body and closing discussion are all present.  Some pagination errors and uneven figure placement prevent the seamless flow required for the top band, yet overall organisation is definitely âwell-organisedâ.
The narrative is clearly driven by the studentâs personal interest in Formula 1 and decisions such as using pre-cap season data show a personal approach that guides the exploration.

annotations.comment: Relocate the page count out of the main text into a header or footer to streamline presentation and avoid administrative details interrupting the narrative.
The title is concise and descriptive, clearly conveying the focus on team spending versus points earned in Formula One.
The narrative effectively conveys genuine personal interest and background in Formula One, enhancing engagement with the exploration.
The stated hypothesis outlines an expected correlation but lacks justification or reference to existing studies; include rationale or preliminary data to support this prediction.
Consistent use of LaTeX for displayed equations enhances readability and presents mathematical expressions with clarity.
The subscript in \(\mu_{\rm v}\) uses Greek nu instead of y; ensure consistent variable naming (e.g., \(\mu_y\)).
Present the calculation of \(\sum (x_i-\mu_x)^2\) step by step alongside the table to demonstrate the standard deviation process with full transparency.
Clarify whether population or sample standard deviation is computed (using denominator \(n\) or \(n-1\)) and justify this choice for the data context.
The formula uses \(\sigma_{\gamma}\) which appears to be a typo; correct it to \(\sigma_x\) for the standard deviation of x-values.
The worked example for the Pearson coefficient shows only one term instead of the full summation; provide a concise summation step to illustrate the complete calculation.
The slope formula reads \(m=r\frac{\sigma_y}{\sigma_y}\), which is incorrect; it should be \(m=r\frac{\sigma_y}{\sigma_x}\).
The works cited list shows inconsistent citation formats and missing URLs for some entries; standardize references according to a recognized style guide.
The contents page lists the appendix, which is outside the assessed scope; consider removing non-assessed sections to improve navigational clarity.
The introductory paragraph is overly long and contains run-on sentences; break it into shorter sentences or separate ideas to improve readability.
The final sentence in the third introductory paragraph is incomplete, leaving the reader without full context; revise to complete the thought.
The background on race structure reiterates common information and could be condensed; focus on only the details directly relevant to the statistical investigation.
Table 1 caption uses a semicolon and informal source citation; standardize caption formatting and citation style for consistency.
Acknowledging the use of all available drivers and teams demonstrates awareness of census data collection, removing need for sampling and enhancing methodological rigor.
Include units in table headers (e.g., âBudget ($M)â) to ensure clarity of the data presented and avoid potential misinterpretation.
Consider summarizing the raw driver salary vs points table with a statistical summary (mean, median, range) to provide insight before moving to graphical representation.
Axes labels in the scatter plot are not sufficiently legible; use higherâresolution images with clear, descriptive axis titles and units.
Figure caption numbering is inconsistent (e.g., âFigure 1.2â without âFigure 1.1â); standardize figure numbering and crossâreferences.
Box plots lack clear axis labels and scale markers; include axis titles with units and visible tick marks to improve interpretation.
When referring to box plots (e.g., âFigure 4.1â), ensure figure numbers match those in captions to avoid confusion.
Show the numeric substitution and intermediate result for the slope calculation (e.g., \(0.9552\times237.1/109.6\)) to improve reproducibility.
The intercept calculation yields \(b\approx-286.372\) not \(-285.972\); recheck algebraic steps to correct this value.
Consider discussing the distinction between correlation and causation here and identify potential confounding factors affecting team performance beyond budget.
When applying the model to 2020 data, reflect on its validity under the new budget cap regulations and discuss any limitations this imposes.
Include quantitative error metrics (e.g., residuals or mean absolute error) when assessing the predictive accuracy of the regression model.
Extending the exploration to 2020 demonstrates initiative; consider outlining further novel extensions or alternative models to deepen personal engagement.
